From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

State government in Australia

This article is about the government of New South Wales. For the political structure of New South Wales, see [Politics of New South Wales](/wiki/Politics_of_New_South_Wales "Politics of New South Wales").

| Government of New South Wales | |
| --- | --- |
| [State government](/wiki/State_government "State government") | |
|  | |
| Overview | |
| Established | * 6 June 1856; 169 years ago (1856-06-06) as a [responsible colonial government](/wiki/Responsible_government "Responsible government") * 1 January 1901; 125 years ago (1901-01-01) as an [Australian state](/wiki/Australian_state "Australian state") |
| State | [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") |
| Country | Australia |
| Leader | [Premier](/wiki/Premier_of_New_South_Wales "Premier of New South Wales") ([Chris Minns](/wiki/Chris_Minns "Chris Minns")) |
| Appointed by | [Governor](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales") ([Margaret Beazley](/wiki/Margaret_Beazley "Margaret Beazley")) on behalf of the [Monarch](/wiki/King_of_Australia "King of Australia") ([Charles III](/wiki/Charles_III "Charles III")) |
| Main organ | * [Executive Council of New South Wales](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_New_South_Wales "Executive Council of New South Wales") (*[de jure](/wiki/De_jure "De jure")*) * Cabinet of New South Wales (*[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")*) |
| Ministries | [11 departments](/wiki/List_of_New_South_Wales_government_agencies "List of New South Wales government agencies") |
| Responsible to | [Parliament of New South Wales](/wiki/Parliament_of_New_South_Wales "Parliament of New South Wales") |
| Annual budget | $120.2 billion[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Headquarters | [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") |
| Website | [nsw.gov.au](http://nsw.gov.au) |

The **Government of New South Wales**, also known as the **NSW Government**, is the executive state government of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), Australia. The government comprises 11 portfolios, led by ministerial departments and supported by several agencies. There are also a number of independent statutory bodies that fall under a portfolio but remain at arms-length for political reasons, such as the [Independent Commission Against Corruption](/wiki/Independent_Commission_Against_Corruption_(New_South_Wales) "Independent Commission Against Corruption (New South Wales)") and the [Electoral Commission](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Electoral_Commission "New South Wales Electoral Commission"). The [Executive Council](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_New_South_Wales "Executive Council of New South Wales"), which consists of the [governor](/wiki/Governor_of_New_South_Wales "Governor of New South Wales") and senior ministers, exercises executive authority through the relevant ministerial portfolios.

The current government is held by the [New South Wales Labor Party](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Labor_Party "New South Wales Labor Party"), led by Premier [Chris Minns](/wiki/Chris_Minns "Chris Minns"). Minns succeeded [Dominic Perrottet](/wiki/Dominic_Perrottet "Dominic Perrottet") of the [Liberal Party](/wiki/Liberal_Party_of_Australia_(New_South_Wales_Division) "Liberal Party of Australia (New South Wales Division)") on 28 March 2023 following that year’s [state election](/wiki/2023_New_South_Wales_state_election "2023 New South Wales state election").

## Ministries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ministries")]

The following individuals serve as government ministers, appointed by the Governor, on behalf of the Monarch, and at the recommendation of the Premier.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The full ministry was announced on 4 April 2023 and was sworn in the following day on 5 April.[[3]](#cite_note-MinnsFullMinistry-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Gazette_2023_2-4) All ministers are members of the ruling Labor Party, while all [shadow ministers](/wiki/Shadow_cabinet "Shadow cabinet") are members of the [opposition in parliament](/wiki/Opposition_(New_South_Wales) "Opposition (New South Wales)").

### Current composition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_New_South_Wales&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Current composition")]

This section is an excerpt from [Minns ministry § Current composition](/wiki/Minns_ministry#Current_composition "Minns ministry").[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Minns_ministry&action=edit)]
